key_insight: In RA patients, plasma proteomics plus machine learning identified a high-performing, interpretable model for predicting future RA-associated interstitial lung disease, with LAG3, NPC2, and LAMP3 emerging as top biomarkers.
clinical_relevance: This work suggests a potential path toward earlier RA-ILD risk stratification, which could help focus monitoring on patients most likely to develop lung disease.
method: Cox proportional hazards analysis of UK Biobank data linked baseline RA to incident ILD risk.
Mendelian randomization tested whether RA may causally increase ILD risk.
Olink plasma proteomics in 781 RA patients was used to train and compare 8 machine learning models, with SHAP used for interpretation.
evidence_signal: The CatBoost model performed best, with AUC 0.884 (95% CI 0.773–0.996), and RA was associated with higher ILD risk in both Cox analysis and Mendelian randomization.
watch_item: The biomarker findings need external validation and prospective testing before they can be used reliably in clinical risk prediction.
paper_title: Development of a Novel Risk Prediction Model for Rheumatoid Arthritis-Associated Interstitial Lung Disease (RA-ILD): A Longitudinal Study
